verdict.summary: Documentation-only change to research/coverage-and-benchmark-plan.md (§1.3, Well-known suites). It replaces the parenthetical 'FedBench/LargeRDFBench are federation, out of single-engine scope' aside with an explicit, honest scoping note: FedBench and LargeRDFBench stay roadmap-only until dedicated adapters, local fixtures, and reproducible result oracles cover their multi-endpoint workloads, and the existing federation-fedshop suite is a smaller, local FedShop-shaped comparison that does not claim coverage of either suite. Verified against the repo: federation-fedshop is a real registered suite (bench/benchmarks.toml, bench/federation/, bench/service-fed-loopback/README.md, bench/CATALOG.md), described consistently as a local FedShop-shaped SERVICE comparison. The inline [SONNET-4.6] attribution markers match the established repo convention, not stray/injection text. The note narrows scope rather than overclaiming; no hard-coded performance numbers, no ZK/MPC/privacy claims, and no correctness/soundness/test-validity/security surface for a markdown-only diff. Nothing instruction-like is embedded in the diff. Identical in substance to the round-2 diff; no findings this round.
